title: linum catharticum var. subalpinum
intro: Linum catharticum var. subalpinum, also known as the subalpine flax, is a plant species within the flax genus, characterized by its slender stems and small, blue-purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Linum catharticum var. subalpinum include its cathartic and emetic effects, making it traditionally used to treat constipation and other digestive issues. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems. Herbal preparations of Linum catharticum var. subalpinum often involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and flowers, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
